---
description: "Use when creating or updating event pages (workshops, training schools, meetings, conferences) for the PalaeOpen website. Covers required frontmatter, file path conventions, auto-categorisation rules, and content structure."
applyTo: "events/**/*.qmd"
---

# Event Pages — PalaeOpen

## File Path Convention

```
events/YYYY/event_name_YYYY.qmd
```

Examples:
- `events/2026/basel_workshop_2026.qmd`
- `events/2026/bulgaria_trainingschool_2026.qmd`
- `events/2025/prague_2025.qmd`

Use `snake_case`, year suffix, no spaces.

## Required Frontmatter

```yaml
---
date: YYYY/MM/DD            # date the page was created or significantly updated
date-format: long
date-modified: last-modified
event-date: YYYY/MM/DD      # first day of the event
event-end-date: YYYY/MM/DD  # last day of the event (include even for single-day events)
description: "One-sentence summary of the event for the listing grid."
sidebar: false
# DO NOT add categories: here — it is set automatically by R/_categorize_events.R
---
```

> **Critical:** Never manually set `categories:` in an event `.qmd` file.
> `R/_categorize_events.R` compares `event-end-date` (or `event-date` if no end date) against the current system date and writes `categories: Future` or `categories: Past` automatically during `quarto render`.
> Any manually set value will be overwritten.

> **Critical:** Never edit `events/events.qmd` by hand.
> It is fully regenerated by `R/_categorize_events.R` on every render.

## Page Title

Use a `##` heading (not `#`) as the main visible heading, immediately after the frontmatter.
Quarto renders the page `title:` field as the `<h1>` — if `title:` is omitted, use `#`.
Avoid duplicating the heading.

Example pattern (no `title:` in frontmatter — heading is the first `##`):

```markdown
---
date: 2026/01/01
date-format: long
date-modified: last-modified
event-date: 2026/05/20
event-end-date: 2026/05/21
description: Workshop on chironomid surface sediment datasets.
sidebar: false
---

## Archiving and Amalgamating Chironomid Surface Sediment Datasets
```

## Standard Content Sections

Adapt to what the issue provides, but follow this order where applicable:

1. **General info callout** (dates, location, fee if any, funding availability)
2. **Rationale / Overview** — Why this event, who it targets
3. **Programme / Schedule** (if provided) — tables or day-by-day breakdowns
4. **Speakers / Instructors** (if applicable)
5. **Objectives** (for training schools)
6. **Important Dates** timeline (registration deadline, notification, etc.)
7. **Applications / Registration** — eligibility, how to apply, form link
8. **Financial Support** — COST Action coverage, daily allowances
9. **Contact** — person name, institution, email (format as plain text, not `mailto:`)

## General Info Callout

Always include a callout at the top with at minimum dates and location:

```markdown
::: {.callout-note}
## 📅 Dates and Location
**Dates:** 20–21 May 2026
**Location:** Basel, Switzerland
:::
```

Add fee and funding lines if present in the issue.

## Registration / Application

- Link to registration forms with a `.btn` button:
  ```markdown
  [Apply here](https://forms.example.com/apply){.btn}
  ```
- State the deadline clearly, either in the callout or in the Applications section.
- If registration is closed or the event is past, note that clearly:
  ```markdown
  > Registration is now closed.
  ```

## Schedule Tables

For detailed schedules, use Markdown tables with emoji icons for activity types (follow the pattern from `events/2025/prague_2025.qmd`):

| Time | Activity | Speaker / Facilitator |
|------|----------|-----------------------|
| 09:00 | 📢 Opening plenary | … |
| 10:00 | 👤 Keynote | … |
| 11:00 | 🎓 Workshop session | … |

Activity icons used in PalaeOpen events:
- 📢 Plenary
- 👤 Keynote
- 🥂 Social
- 🗨️ Parallel discussion
- 🎓 Training / workshop

## Materials and Images

Place event-specific materials (flyers, logos) under:
```
Materials/[event_subfolder]/
```

Reference with a path relative to the repo root:
```markdown
![Flyer](Materials/flyers/basel_2026_flyer.png)
```

Confirm the file exists in the repository before referencing it.
